mission.title: Install and Configure Cluster API (CAPI)
mission.description: Cluster API (CAPI) is a Kubernetes operator that provides declarative APIs and tooling to simplify the provisioning, upgrading, and management of multiple Kubernetes clusters. It is particularly useful for platform operators looking to automate cluster lifecycle management across various infrastructure environments. Key trade-offs include the complexity of initial setup versus the long-term benefits of automated cluster management.

steps.title: Add the Cluster API Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Cluster API Helm repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install the necessary components.

```bash

helm repo add cluster-api https://charts.cluster-api.sigs.k8s.io

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the Cluster API chart repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the Cluster API components
steps.description:
This step installs the Cluster API components into the specified namespace.

```bash

kubectl create namespace cluster-api-system

helm install cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace cluster-api-system --version v1.12.4

```

> Creates a namespace for Cluster API components.
> Installs Cluster API with the specified version.
commands: helm install cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace cluster-api-system --version v1.12.4

steps.title: Install the necessary CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions required for Cluster API to function properly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/cluster-api-components.yaml

```

> Applies the CRDs for Cluster API.

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
This step checks that the Cluster API components are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n cluster-api-system

kubectl get crd

```

> Lists the pods in the cluster-api-system namespace to verify they are running.
> Lists the installed CRDs to ensure they are present.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Cluster API
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Cluster API components and CRDs. Be aware that this may lead to data loss if any clusters managed by CAPI are still present.

```bash

helm uninstall cluster-api --namespace cluster-api-system

kubectl delete namespace cluster-api-system

```

> Uninstalls the Cluster API Helm release.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Cluster API
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup any important data. This step upgrades the Cluster API components to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n cluster-api-system -o json > backup-cluster-api-pods.json

helm upgrade cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace cluster-api-system --version v1.12.4

kubectl get pods -n cluster-api-system

```

> Backup current pod states.
> Upgrades Cluster API to the specified version.
> Verifies that the upgraded pods are running.
commands: kubectl get pods -n cluster-api-system -o json > backup-cluster-api-pods.json
helm upgrade cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace cluster-api-system --version v1.12.4

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to install CRDs'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The CRDs may not be compatible with the current Kubernetes version.

**Fix:**
Ensure you are using compatible CRD versions for your Kubernetes cluster.

**Affected versions:** 1.26+

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Pod crashloopbackoff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** One or more Cluster API components may have configuration issues.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the pods using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n cluster-api-system' for more details.

**Affected versions:** 1.26+

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Your kubeconfig may not be set up correctly or the API server is down.

**Fix:**
Verify your kubeconfig file and ensure the Kubernetes API server is running.

**Affected versions:** 1.26+

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'No resources found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Cluster API components may not have been installed correctly.

**Fix:**
Re-run the installation commands and check for errors during installation.

**Affected versions:** 1.26+

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new infrastructure providers and improved the stability of existing components.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated certain flags related to legacy infrastructure providers.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.11.x, ensure to review the new CRD specifications.

resolution.summary: You should see the Cluster API pods running with a status of 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get pods -n cluster-api-system'.
codeSnippets: helm install cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace cluster-api-system --version v1.12.4

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the specified resources.
